data.title: Fudan Zhangjiang Boosts Yield on Idle Cash With RMB210 Million SPD Bank Deposit
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co ( (HK:1349) ).

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co has subscribed to a principal-guaranteed structured deposit product with SPD Bank, investing RMB210 million of idle self-owned funds generated from daily operations. The 88-day product is assessed as low-risk, offers a floating annualized return between 1.00% and 1.95%, and links its yield to EUR/USD movements, providing the company with modest income on surplus cash.

Under Hong Kong Listing Rules, the size of this investment causes one or more applicable percentage ratios to exceed 5% but remain below 25%, classifying the transaction as a disclosable transaction requiring reporting and public announcement without shareholder approval. The move underscores the company’s conservative treasury management, balancing capital preservation with enhanced returns, and signals disciplined liquidity deployment for stakeholders monitoring its financial strategy.

More about Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co operates in the biopharmaceutical industry, focusing on the research, development and commercialization of pharmaceutical products. The company uses financial instruments to manage its liquidity, allocating idle operational funds into low-risk, principal-guaranteed structured deposit products to optimize returns while preserving capital.

Its market focus includes maintaining a stable financial position to support ongoing operations and investments in drug development. By engaging in short-term, low-risk banking products, the company seeks incremental yield on surplus cash without compromising liquidity or risk management standards.

Average Trading Volume: 2,680,614

Current Market Cap: HK$772.6M

The latest update is out from Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:0923) ).

Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited has announced a leadership transition effective 1 September 2026, with long-serving executive director and chief executive officer Mr. Lam King Sang retiring from all his roles in the group to devote more time to personal commitments. The board emphasized there is no disagreement with Mr. Lam and expressed appreciation for his decade of service, signaling a planned and orderly handover rather than a dispute-driven change.

On the same effective date, the company will elevate its chief financial officer and executive director, Mr. Tam Sui Kin, Chris, to the role of chief executive officer, leveraging his more than 35 years of experience in auditing, accounting, project financing and financial management. The move places a seasoned finance professional at the helm, which may influence the group’s strategic focus on financial discipline and capital management as it navigates future growth and industry challenges.

More about Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited

Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed environmental services company focused on integrated waste management solutions through its group of subsidiaries. The company operates in the environmental and recycling sector, providing waste treatment and related services to support sustainable resource management in Hong Kong and the region.

data.description:

CGN Power Co ( (HK:1816) ) just unveiled an announcement.

CGN Power Co., Ltd. has announced an adjustment to the conversion price of its A Share convertible corporate bonds following the approval of its 2025 profit distribution plan. The RMB4.9 billion bonds, issued in July 2025 to non-specific investors and traded on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, initially carried a conversion price of RMB3.67 per share.

To maintain the dividend amount per share after declaring a cash dividend of RMB0.086 per share for 2025, the company is revising the bond conversion price in line with the prospectus formula for corporate actions affecting share capital. As a result, the conversion price will be reduced from RMB3.67 to RMB3.58 per share, effective July 10, 2026, which may slightly enhance the attractiveness of conversion for bondholders while preserving equitable treatment among shareholders.

The company operates in the power sector, focusing on nuclear power generation and related energy services for both A-share and H-share investors in the Chinese capital markets.

Its securities trade on multiple exchanges, including A Share convertible corporate bonds listed on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, giving the firm diversified access to domestic and international investors. data.description:

Abbisko Cayman Limited ( (HK:2256) ) has shared an announcement.

Abbisko Therapeutics has entered a strategic collaboration with AstraZeneca to conduct a multicenter Phase I/II clinical trial combining lumipodlin, a first‑in‑class oral PD‑L1 inhibitor, with AstraZeneca’s EGFR‑TKI Tagrisso in patients with EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive locally advanced or metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. The investigational new drug application for this combination was cleared by China’s National Medical Products Administration, positioning Abbisko to lead the Phase II study and share trial responsibilities with AstraZeneca, potentially strengthening its clinical pipeline in a high‑need NSCLC segment while highlighting the promise of oral immune‑oncology agents alongside established targeted therapies.

The company is developing lumipodlin (ABSK043), a novel orally bioavailable small‑molecule PD‑L1 inhibitor aimed at treating non‑small cell lung cancer and addressing unmet needs in EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive NSCLC.

CALB Group has further amended its 2024–2026 Engineering and Construction Framework Agreement with Jiangsu Chengdong Construction, a connected party, by entering into a third supplemental agreement to raise the annual spending cap for 2026. SSY Group has become the first entity in mainland China to secure National Medical Products Administration approval for the production and registration of 0.5g Methocarbamol Tablets, a muscle relaxant used to treat joint and muscle sprains, lumbar muscle strain and sciatica. The drug’s consistency evaluation status underscores the group’s capability to meet stringent regulatory standards in the competitive generic medicines market.

The company also obtained NMPA registration approval for Phloroglucinol Dihydrate as a bulk drug, following its earlier clearance for Phloroglucinol Injection, enabling broader use in preparations targeting acute spasmodic pain in digestive, urinary and gynecological systems. InSilico Medicine has entered a strategic collaboration with Japanese biopharmaceutical group Takeda to deploy its end-to-end AI drug discovery platform, Pharma.AI, to identify novel, clinically differentiated drug candidates across Takeda’s core therapeutic areas. Under the agreement, Insilico will lead AI-driven molecular discovery, while Takeda will take selected candidates through global clinical development, gaining exclusive worldwide rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize any resulting therapeutics.

The deal provides Insilico with about USD60 million in upfront and near-term payments and could reach a total value of up to USD600 million through success-based milestones, plus tiered royalties on future product sales. The partnership underscores growing pharma demand for AI-enabled R&D, strengthens Insilico’s positioning as a key AI partner to large drugmakers, and signals potential long-term upside for the company’s revenue streams and shareholders if the collaboration yields commercially successful medicines.

Through its proprietary Pharma.AI platform and in-house R&D capabilities, it develops drug candidates targeting unmet medical needs in areas such as fibrosis, oncology, immunology, pain, and obesity and metabolic disorders, while also extending its AI technologies to advanced materials, agriculture, nutritional products, and veterinary medicine.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited ( (HK:0474) ) has provided an announcement.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited has announced it will miss the 30 June 2026 deadline to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026, citing the need for additional time to collect valuation data for certain financial assets, associates and investment property required by its auditor. The board has decided not to issue unaudited management accounts to avoid confusing investors, and stresses that group operations remain normal while it works with the auditor to complete remaining procedures and release the results as soon as practicable.

Because of the delay in audited results, the company expects it may also miss the 31 July 2026 deadline to dispatch its 2026 annual report, which would put it in non-compliance with listing rules until a new date is announced. The board meeting originally scheduled for 30 June 2026 to approve the results has been postponed, and trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 2 July 2026 pending publication of the overdue annual results, a move that directly affects shareholder liquidity and market perception of the group’s governance and reporting reliability.

An update from Hao Tian International Construction Investment Group Limited ( (HK:1341) ) is now available.

Hao Tian International Construction Investment Group Limited has announced it will not be able to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026 by the 30 June regulatory deadline, citing the need for additional time to complete valuations of certain financial assets, associates and prepayments required for the audit. The board has decided against releasing unaudited management accounts to avoid potentially confusing investors, and while it stresses that the group’s operations remain normal, it cannot provide a firm timetable for completion and will update shareholders in due course.

The board meeting originally scheduled for 30 June 2026 to approve the results has been postponed, and trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 2 July 2026 pending publication of the overdue annual results, heightening uncertainty for investors until audited figures are available.
